subject: Physics
title: Optics
outline: Reflection at plane and curved surfaces

modules.title: Nature and rectilinear propagation of light
modules.html:
Light is an EM wave; it travels in straight lines in a uniform medium — hence shadows, eclipses and the pinhole camera.

Umbra: fully dark region. Penumbra: partially shaded region.

examples.q: During a solar eclipse, what is between Sun and Earth?
A pinhole camera produces images that are

modules.title: Reflection
modules.html:
Laws: (1) angle of incidence = angle of reflection; (2) incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in one plane.

Plane mirror: image is virtual, erect, laterally inverted, same size, same distance behind the mirror.

Curved mirrors: concave (converging) forms real or virtual images; convex (diverging) forms virtual, upright, diminished images.

Mirror formula: 1/f = 1/u + 1/v; magnification m = v/u = image height / object height. Sign convention: distances measured from mirror; real +, virtual −. (Adopt textbook convention.)

examples.q: Object 20 cm from concave mirror of f=10 cm. Image distance?

modules.title: Refraction and total internal reflection
modules.html:
Snell's law: n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂. Refractive index n = c/v = sin i / sin r.

Critical angle c: for a ray from denser to less dense medium, at c the refracted ray goes along the surface. sin c = 1/n. If i > c → total internal reflection.

Applications: optical fibres, prism binoculars, mirages (hot air acts as a less dense medium).

examples.q: Light passes from air into glass (n=1.5) at 30°. Refraction angle?

modules.title: Lenses
modules.html:
Converging (convex): parallel rays converge to F. Diverging (concave): parallel rays appear to come from F.

Lens formula 1/f = 1/v − 1/u (using real-is-positive Cartesian convention: with u negative for real objects). Magnification m = v/u.

Power P = 1/f (in metres), unit dioptre (D).

examples.q: Convex lens f=10 cm, object 15 cm away. Image?
examples.a: 1/v = 1/10 + 1/(−15)? Using 1/f = 1/v + 1/u (object distance positive) → v = 30 cm, real, inverted, magnified.

examples.q: Power of a lens with f=25 cm?

modules.title: Optical instruments and eye defects
modules.html:
• Camera: converging lens forms real, inverted, diminished image on film/sensor.
  • Simple microscope (magnifying glass): converging lens, object inside f, image virtual, magnified.
  • Compound microscope: two convex lenses (objective + eyepiece).
  • Astronomical telescope: two convex lenses, objective long f, eyepiece short f.

Eye defects

  • Short sight (myopia): can't see far. Corrected with a diverging (concave) lens.
  • Long sight (hypermetropia): can't see near. Corrected with a converging (convex) lens.
  • Presbyopia: age-related loss of accommodation. Bifocals.
  • Astigmatism: cornea not spherical. Corrected with cylindrical lens.

examples.q: What image does a camera lens form?
